title: Bachmannia woodii

The genus Bachmannia is a monotypic genus, meaning it only contains the single species Bachmannia woodii. intro: Bachmannia woodii, also known as Wood's Alder, is a woody shrub native to South Africa, belonging to the family Icacinaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties.
